manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: serenity, vigilante, bathtub, daybreak, apartment, snowman, prairie, drugstore, stairway.
Your opponent's team words are: psychopath, statute, mutt, enzyme, pond, census, hepatitis, egghead.
Distractor words are: stewardess, blimp, appetizer, pharmacist, prom, geisha, shaman.
The kill words are: cowardice.

message.reasoning:
We need to give a clue related to some of our team's words. Need to avoid clue being any of the words themselves or containing parts. Provide CLUE: WORD, TARGETS: list.

We must not give any other text.

We must choose prudent clue: perhaps "morning" relates to daybreak, maybe also serenity? Could also relate: "cold" to snowman, prairie, maybe bathtub? "bath" relates to bathtub but also maybe not others. Need to avoid hitting opponent words? Our clue shouldn't be ambiguous with opponent/distractor words. The clue word must not be any of the listed words. 'cold' not in list. Could affect snowman and prairie (cold). Also maybe drugstore? no. Serenity maybe "calm". "calm" relates to serenity, maybe also vigilante? No. "night" opposite daybreak not good. "winter" gives snowman, maybe also prairie? Could be. But also "drugstore" possibly "pharmacy" but that is a distractor. So avoid.

Better to pick "dawn" but that's similar to daybreak; but would also maybe hint at serenity? Not sure. "snow" relates to snowman, maybe also prairie (snow prairie). Might be safe.

We need to list the target words we intend. Could give clue "cold" and target "snowman, prairie". Also maybe "bathtub" cold water? But might be ambiguous. "cold" could also evoke "psychopath"? Not really. "cold" may hint to "statute"? no. "cold" might not be harmful.

Could also do "building": apartment, stairway, maybe drugstore. But "building" could also hint to "stewardess"? no.

I think "building" would link apartment, stairway, drugstore (store building). But also could link "blimp"? no. "building" not in opponent list.

Check kill word: cowardice - ensure clue not that.

I'll choose "building". TARGETS: apartment, stairway, drugstore.

Clue must be single word. Provide.

Thus answer: 
CLUE: building
TARGETS: apartment, stairway, drugstore

Check that "building" does not contain parts of any listed words. It's fine.

Return as required
